Title: Roles of RIN and ethylene in tomato fruit ripening and ripeningassociated traits

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: fruit color and nutritional quality; carotenoid-derived aroma precursor pool
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: core carotenoid pathway, committed entry step
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: condenses two molecules of geranylgeranyl pyrophosphate to form phytoene
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced PSY1 transcript accumulation in RIN-deficient fruit is associated with strongly decreased carotenoid accumulation; ethylene treatment partially induces PSY1 and pigment accumulation but not to WT levels.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: ripening fruit; induced during breaker and post-breaker stages; reduced in RIN-deficient fruit and induced by exogenous ethylene
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; HPLC carotenoid profiling; ethylene/1-MCP treatment comparison in WT and RIN-CRISPR fruit
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: RIN (regulatory association inferred from transcript dependence)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to improve tomato carotenoid accumulation, but high carotenoid accumulation also requires intact RIN-dependent ripening program.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PSY1 transcript was inhibited by about 90% in RIN-deficient fruits. 2. Low PSY1 expression coincided with >10-fold lower lycopene and generally impaired carotenoid accumulation. 3. Ethylene increased PSY1 transcripts in RIN-CRISPR fruits, showing partial RIN-independent induction.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Measured as a key downstream pathway gene, not as the main experimental gene.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CHY transcripts were strongly reduced in RIN-deficient fruits and partially induced by ethylene, consistent with impaired carotenoid branch metabolism in the absence of RIN.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: ripening fruit; reduced in RIN-deficient fruit; induced by exogenous ethylene
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: RIN (regulatory association inferred from transcript dependence)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for tuning carotenoid branch composition, but effect depends on ripening regulatory background.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CHY/SlBCH2 transcript was inhibited by about 80% in RIN-deficient fruit. 2. Ethylene increased CHY transcript in RIN-CRISPR fruits. 3. Results support RIN dependence for full carotenoid branch gene expression.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Supportive pathway gene assayed in Results.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: fruit pigmentation and nutritional carotenoid accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: isomerizes cis-carotene intermediates toward the all-trans lycopene form
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CRTISO transcripts were reduced by about 75% in RIN-deficient fruit, matching severe impairment of carotenoid accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: ripening fruit; reduced in RIN-deficient fruit
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: RIN (regulatory association inferred from transcript dependence)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential carotenoid engineering target but insufficient alone without ripening regulators.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CRTISO transcript was substantially reduced in RIN-deficient fruits. 2. Reduced CRTISO expression accompanied severe carotenoid deficiency. 3. Supports RIN requirement for full activation of carotenoid pathway genes.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Supportive pathway gene assayed in Results.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: converts S-adenosyl-L-methionine to ACC, the direct precursor of ethylene
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ACS2 transcript levels were <20% of WT in RIN-deficient fruits, consistent with failure to induce autocatalytic system-2 ethylene production.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: ripening fruit; strongly induced in WT during ripening; reduced in RIN-deficient fruit
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: fruit ripening stage; propylene challenge for system-2 induction
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; GC measurement of ethylene; propylene induction assay
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: RIN (reported as direct target in cited studies)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important target for modulating climacteric ripening intensity and timing.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced ethylene can impair full ripening, color, and aroma.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ACS2 transcripts were greatly reduced in RIN-deficient fruits. 2. RIN-deficient fruit failed to induce system-2 ethylene synthesis. 3. ACS2 is implicated as part of the RIN-dependent autocatalytic ethylene module.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Assayed as a key downstream biosynthetic gene, not the primary manipulated gene.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ACS4 transcripts were <20% of WT in RIN-deficient fruits, associated with defective system-2 ethylene burst.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: ripening fruit; strongly induced in WT ripening; reduced in RIN-deficient fruit
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; GC ethylene profiling; propylene induction assay
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: RIN (reported as direct target in cited studies)
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Suppression may compromise full ripening and fruit quality.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ACS4 transcript accumulation was strongly reduced in RIN-deficient fruit. 2. Loss of ACS4 induction is linked to failure of autocatalytic system-2 ethylene synthesis. 3. Supports a RIN-dependent ethylene biosynthetic loop.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ACO1 transcript levels reached only about 40% of WT in RIN-deficient fruits, contributing to strongly reduced ethylene production.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: ripening fruit; induced in WT; reduced in RIN-deficient fruit
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; GC ethylene profiling; propylene induction assay
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: RIN (reported as direct target in cited studies)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to modulate ripening ethylene output.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Lower ACO1 may weaken ripening completion and aroma/color development.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ACO1 transcript was reduced to about 40% of WT in RIN-deficient fruit. 2. Reduced ACO1 expression accompanies major deficiency in climacteric ethylene production. 3. Supports RIN-dependent activation of the system-2 module.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: catalyzes reversible transamination in branched-chain amino acid metabolism linked to volatile formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: BCAT1 transcripts fell to 7.9% and 9.0% of WT in RIN-deficient fruits, coinciding with strong reduction in amino acid-derived aroma volatiles.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: ripening fruit; sharply reduced in RIN-deficient fruit
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: RIN (reported as direct target in cited studies)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for improving flavor volatiles, but dependent on intact ripening regulation.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Flavor improvement may couple with ripening changes.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BCAT1 expression was severely reduced in RIN-deficient fruit. 2. This reduction paralleled decreases in amino acid-derived volatiles. 3. Supports RIN contribution to flavor volatile biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: oxygenates polyunsaturated fatty acids to hydroperoxides that feed volatile production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: LoxC transcripts dropped to 5.4% and 3.5% of WT in RIN-deficient fruits, matching strong reduction in lipid-derived volatiles.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: ripening fruit; strongly reduced in RIN-deficient fruit
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key pathway step for C5/C6 volatile formation
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: RIN (reported as direct target in cited studies)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Flavor engineering target for C5/C6 volatiles.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LoxC transcript abundance was dramatically reduced in RIN-deficient fruit. 2. Lipid-derived volatiles were also strongly reduced. 3. Indicates RIN is important for volatile pathway activation.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: oxygenates polyunsaturated fatty acids to hydroperoxide intermediates for volatile formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: LoxB transcripts were reduced to 6.9% and 8.0% of WT in RIN-deficient fruits, associated with impaired lipid-derived volatile production.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: ripening fruit; strongly reduced in RIN-deficient fruit
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LoxB expression was markedly reduced in RIN-deficient fruit. 2. Reduced expression accompanied lower lipid-derived volatile levels. 3. Supports RIN-dependent control of flavor volatile metabolism.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: cleaves fatty acid hydroperoxides to produce volatile aldehydes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: HPL transcript levels were reduced to 41.4% and 48.5% of WT in RIN-deficient fruit, accompanying lower lipid-derived volatiles.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: ripening fruit; reduced in RIN-deficient fruit
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: RIN (reported as direct target in cited studies)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HPL transcripts were reduced in RIN-deficient fruits. 2. Lipid-derived volatile accumulation was impaired. 3. Results support RIN contribution to volatile-forming enzyme expression.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: aldehyde-to-alcohol conversion in lipid-derived volatile pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: reduces aldehydes to corresponding alcohols in volatile biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ADH2 transcripts were moderately reduced in RIN-deficient fruits, accompanying reduced volatile production.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: ripening fruit; reduced in RIN-deficient fruit
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ADH2 transcript levels were reduced to about 70% of WT in RIN-deficient fruit. 2. Lower ADH2 was associated with reduced volatile output. 3. Indicates broad RIN dependence of aroma pathway gene expression.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: transfers acyl groups to alcohols to form volatile esters
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: AAT1 transcripts fell to 10.4% and 6.6% of WT in RIN-deficient fruit, corresponding to reduced volatile ester production.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: ripening fruit; strongly reduced in RIN-deficient fruit
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: RIN (reported as direct target in cited studies)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for enhancing tomato aroma esters.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AAT1 expression was severely reduced in RIN-deficient fruit. 2. Volatile production was broadly reduced. 3. Supports a role of RIN in activating ester biosynthesis genes.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: deaminates phenylalanine to cinnamic acid, initiating benzenoid/phenylpropanoid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PAL3 transcript levels were reduced to 43.8% and 33.9% of WT in RIN-deficient fruits, consistent with lower phenylalanine-derived volatile accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: ripening fruit; reduced in RIN-deficient fruit
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Flavor engineering target for benzenoid volatiles.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PAL3 transcript accumulation was reduced in RIN-deficient fruit. 2. Phenylalanine-derived volatile compounds were lower. 3. Indicates RIN contributes to benzenoid volatile precursor supply.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: plant hormone; carotenoid; volatile aroma compounds
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: controls climacteric ripening progression and fruit quality traits including color and aroma
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation of ripening genes; required for full expression of system-2 ethylene, carotenoid, volatile and some cell wall genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: major ripening progression regulator required for full carotenoid accumulation, volatile production, and autocatalytic system-2 ethylene synthesis, but not sufficient/required for ripening initiation
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: ACS2; ACS4; ACO1; PSY1; CHY/SlBCH2; CRTISO; BCAT1; LoxC; HPL; AAT1; multiple cell wall genes including CEL2, EXP1, PL, XYL1, Mside1, PG, TBG4, MAN4a
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CRISPR loss of RIN allowed ripening initiation at normal timing but fruits produced only ~10% WT carotenoid and ethylene levels, had strongly reduced volatiles, failed to induce system-2 ethylene and never completed full ripening even with exogenous ethylene.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: ripening fruit; induced by ethylene according to this paper's discussion and cited previous work
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: fruit ripening stage; exogenous ethylene; propylene; 1-MCP
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout lines; Western blot; ethylene measurements by GC; propylene induction assay; 1-MCP and ethylene treatment; HPLC carotenoid profiling; GC/GC-MS volatile profiling; qRT-PCR
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates or is required for high-level expression of ethylene biosynthesis, carotenoid biosynthesis, volatile biosynthesis, and subsets of cell wall genes; may repress XTH5 and XTH8
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: RIN loss causes severe reduction in color, aroma, and completion of ripening despite extensive softening.
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: participates in positive feedback with ethylene; may participate with TAGL1 in positive feedback promoting system-2 ethylene
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: reported interaction/complex formation with TAGL1; possible heterodimers with FUL1/2 and AGL11; interaction with NAC4
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: biosynthesis of ethylene, carotenoids, aroma volatiles, and regulation of cell wall remodeling during ripening
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating RIN can alter shelf life, but full loss strongly compromises carotenoid accumulation and flavor; therefore not ideal alone for quality improvement.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RIN-deficient fruits initiated ripening at about the same time as WT, showing RIN is not essential for ripening initiation. 2. RIN is required for autocatalytic system-2 ethylene synthesis, full carotenoid accumulation, and volatile production. 3. Exogenous ethylene cannot restore full ripening in RIN-deficient fruit, indicating RIN and ethylene act together for completion of ripening.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper contrasts true RIN deficiency with the classical rin mutant, whose RIN-MC fusion protein acts as a transcriptional repressor.
